methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Emerald AI, a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Varun Sivaram, has raised $24.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private frontier-tech companies in climate and energy (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy, and others) plus 1 public company (LNZA), providing relevant benchmarks for early-stage venture assessment in this sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $24.5M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $61M valuation. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $175M, with a range spanning $110M (Steady Energy) to $1.8B (Watershed). The latter range reflects material heterogeneity in the peer set, though the median is grounded in observed transaction data.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $52M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $118M. The wide spread between methods reflects tension between Emerald AI's current capital raised and peer valuations at more advanced stages. Key uncertainties include whether Emerald AI's technology differentiation and market position justify series A-stage pricing, and whether the peer set—which spans two orders of magnitude—adequately controls for company-specific technical and commercial risk. A pre-seed or seed valuation closer to the $52M–$61M range appears more defensible absent additional operational milestones.
